commit.message:
Build plugin in bookworm container to match bifrost's dynamic image (#4)

* Initial plan

* fix: build plugin in Alpine container to match bifrost's build environment

The plugin was failing to load with 'plugin was built with a different
version of package encoding' because it was built on Ubuntu (glibc)
while bifrost is built in Alpine (musl). This changes the CI workflow
to build the plugin inside golang:1.26.1-alpine3.23 Docker container
(matching bifrost's Dockerfile) and updates the Makefile's Docker build
target to use Alpine instead of Debian bookworm.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/lukoerfer/bifrost-encoding-format-plugin/sessions/ff260fe6-a414-454f-b29f-e98660838cd3

Co-authored-by: lukoerfer <10530659+lukoerfer@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use bookworm base image to match bifrost's dynamic build environment

The bifrost dynamic Dockerfile uses golang:1.26.1-bookworm (glibc),
not Alpine (musl). Reverts the CI workflow and Makefile Docker build
target to use bookworm with apt-get package installation.

commit.message:
[analysis] Paper 8: Kac identifiability scaffold — inverse solver, tests, LaTeX (#198)

Scaffold for Paper 8: 'Can You Hear the Shape of an Organ?'

New module src/analytical/kac_identifiability.py:
- compute_jacobian() with scaled/raw modes
- jacobian_condition_number() using dimensionless sensitivity matrix
- condition_number_map() for parameter-space sweeps
- invert_frequencies() with normalised Newton-Raphson solver
- identifiability_analysis() with Fisher information and Cramer-Rao bounds
- sphere_vs_oblate_comparison() showing sphere rank deficiency

Key results (all verified by 31 passing tests):
- Sphere scaled condition number: ~1.1e10 (rank deficient)
- Oblate Ritz scaled condition number: ~73 (full rank, well-conditioned)
- Round-trip inversion recovers (a, c, E) to <1% from 10% perturbation
- Cramer-Rao bounds finite for oblate, infinite for sphere

LaTeX skeleton (elsarticle, targeting Inverse Problems / Proc Roy Soc A):
- 6 section files: intro, theory, methodology, results, discussion, conclusion
- references.bib with Kac 1966, Gordon/Webb/Wolpert 1992, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[paper] Write full content for Paper 7 results, validation, formulation, and discussion sections (#193)

Results section:
- Forward model predictions for 4 ripeness stages (f2=203.2 Hz unripe to 46.4 Hz overripe)
- f2^2 linearity verification (alpha=161.5 Hz^2/MPa, CoV=0.07%)
- Inversion roundtrip validation (<0.01% error)
- Sobol sensitivity analysis (E dominates with ST=0.54, then a with ST=0.38)
- Universal ripeness curve (Pi_ripe=0.062, CoV=3.3% across 4 cultivars)
- Multi-cultivar comparison (Crimson Sweet, Sugar Baby, Charleston Gray, Seedless)
- Parametric ripening sweep (239 Hz to 36 Hz over 20 stages)

Validation section:
- Yamamoto et al. (1980) watermelon comparison table
- De Belie et al. (2000) and Chen & De Baerdemaeker (1993) cross-species
- Monte Carlo geometric uncertainty (CoV=13.5% for ±10% geometry error)
- Individual parameter sensitivity analysis

Formulation section:
- Canonical parameter table
- Explicit stiffness decomposition (bending + membrane + pre-stress)
- Alpha=161.5 Hz^2/MPa derivation
- Explicit inversion formula with round-trip error bound
- Pi_ripe redefined to match code implementation

Discussion section:
- Quantitative ripening physics (factor 4.4 frequency drop)
- Biomedical FSI connection with specific numbers
- Updated limitations with Sobol-backed uncertainty bounds
- Practical implications with MC uncertainty quantification
- New generality subsection

All values computed from actual model runs, not estimated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[paper] Fix watermelon E_rind values: 25x increase to match literature tap-tone frequencies (#188)

Previous E_rind values (0.8-8 MPa) produced f2 = 11-41 Hz, far below the
published range of 60-250 Hz. Watermelon rind is cellulose-rich structural
tissue with E = 15-200 MPa.

Updated canonical parameters:
  unripe:   E = 200 MPa -> f2 = 203 Hz (lit: 140-250 Hz)
  turning:  E = 120 MPa -> f2 = 147 Hz (lit: 100-180 Hz)
  ripe:     E =  50 MPa -> f2 =  90 Hz (lit:  80-160 Hz)
  overripe: E =  15 MPa -> f2 =  46 Hz (lit:  40-80  Hz)

Also updated:
- ripeness_from_modulus() thresholds (25/80/160 MPa boundaries)
- parametric_ripening_sweep() E range (250-10 MPa)
- Sobol sensitivity bounds (10-250 MPa)
- All 61 regression tests re-pinned to new values
- README canonical parameter table

All 333 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Stateless backend with Redis persistence, cookie session, GDPR banner

API:
- Replace in-memory ConcurrentHashMap with Spring Data Redis
- Game state stored as JSON with 30-minute TTL (auto-expiry)
- RedisConfig with Jackson serializer and default typing
- GameState implements Serializable, @JsonIgnoreProperties
- Redis host/port configurable via REDIS_HOST/REDIS_PORT env vars
- Tests updated to mock RedisTemplate with in-memory HashMap

UI:
- Install js-cookie; store gameId in hexle_game_id cookie (30min)
- On app load: resume existing game from cookie, fall back to new game
- resumeGameThunk added to gameSlice for session restoration
- GDPR cookie consent banner (Accept/Decline, localStorage backed)
- ApiGameState type added for API response shape

Kubernetes:
- Add Redis Deployment (redis:7-alpine, AOF persistence, LRU eviction)
- Add Redis ClusterIP Service on port 6379
- Add PVC using nfs-client StorageClass (1Gi)
- API deployment scaled back to replicas: 2 (now stateless)
- API service: remove sessionAffinity (no longer needed)
- API deployment: add REDIS_HOST/REDIS_PORT env vars

commit.message:
[paper] Fix 4 minor issues from Reviewer B Round 2 (Paper 6) (#187)

m1: Change 'minimum 5.8x' to '5th-percentile 5.8x' in UQ section —
    the MC minimum is 4.97x, not 5.8x; the 5th percentile is correct.

m2: Add RMS-vs-peak note in Methods §2.7 — floor displacements are RMS
    (from power balance); perception thresholds are peak (via sqrt(2)).
    Comparison is conservative by sqrt(2) (~41%).

m3: Add paragraph in Results §3.5 justifying fixed MC parameters
    (T=1.8, eta_f=0.01): correlated with zeta_floor, narrow literature
    range, factor-of-two variation already encompassed by damping sweep.

m4: Add 'approximately' qualifier to '2.5 orders' in Discussion §4.1
    (actual value 2.46; other instances already qualified).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[paper] Scaffold Paper 7: watermelon ripeness acoustic assessment (#185)

Add analytical module, comprehensive tests, and project README for
Paper 7 — non-destructive acoustic assessment of watermelon ripeness
via oblate spheroidal shell resonance inversion.

New files:
- src/analytical/watermelon_model.py: forward model, closed-form
  inversion, universal ripeness curve, parametric sweeps, Sobol
  sensitivity, multi-cultivar comparison, De Belie validation
- tests/test_watermelon.py: 61 tests (59 fast + 2 slow/Sobol)
- projects/watermelon-ripeness/README.md: project overview

Key results (canonical ripe watermelon, E=2 MPa):
- f₂ = 18.08 Hz, Q = 6.67
- Inversion roundtrip error: 0.0000% (exact)
- Pi_ripe collapses across cultivars: 0.061 +/- 8.6%
- Sobol: E dominates (S_T=0.54), then geometry (a=0.38)
- All 333 tests pass (272 existing + 61 new)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 404 on guess: scale API to 1 replica, add session affinity

In-memory ConcurrentHashMap game state cannot be shared across pods.
With 2 replicas and round-robin LB, start-game hits pod A but the
first guess hits pod B which has no state, causing 404 every time.

- api-deployment: replicas 2 → 1
- api-service: add sessionAffinity: ClientIP (1hr timeout) as safety net
  for when replicas are scaled up in future with a shared state backend.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(identity): add SeedDefaultRolePermissions feature and IPermissionResolver DB-first resolution

- Create SeedDefaultRolePermissions command that populates iam_role_permissions
  table from RolePermissionCatalog defaults when no system-level mappings exist
- Add POST /identity/role-permissions/seed-defaults endpoint (PlatformAdmin only)
- Create IPermissionResolver abstraction with DB-first, static-fallback strategy
- Implement PermissionResolver in Infrastructure layer
- Refactor 6 files to use IPermissionResolver instead of static Role.GetPermissionsForRole():
  - LoginResponseBuilder (now async CreateLoginResponseAsync)
  - SelectTenant handler
  - GetCurrentUser handler
  - ListRoles handler
  - IdentityModuleService (cross-module query)
  - CreateDelegation handler
- Update ILoginResponseBuilder interface to async
- Register IPermissionResolver in DI
- Update all affected tests to pass IPermissionResolver mock

RolePermissionCatalog and Role.GetPermissionsForRole() preserved as fallback.

[infra] Fix CI \Bbbk clash: newtxmath vs amssymb on TeX Live (#179)

All 7 .tex files: split amsmath,amssymb and add \let\Bbbk\relax
before amssymb to prevent fatal redefinition error on TeX Live.
MiKTeX treated this as a warning; TeX Live (CI) treats it as fatal.

commit.message:
Consolidate same-day inspections into a single entry

Key inspections by date only instead of date+type so that multiple
inspection types on the same day (e.g. a scored Pre-permit inspection
plus an Administrative one) merge into one entry. This matches what
ABCEats does and avoids confusing duplicate rows in the search grid.
